cases.passage: A Physical Education teacher, Mrs. Rao, was conducting a basketball skills assessment for her students. She had to measure the accuracy of free throws for 20 students. She decided to use a percentage score to evaluate their performance. To calculate the percentage score, she recorded the total number of free throws made by each student and divided it by the total number of attempts. The scores ranged from 60% to 90%. Mrs. Rao wanted to know how she could improve her assessment methods to make it more accurate and reliable. She also wanted to compare the performance of her students with national standards.

questions.q: What type of score did Mrs. Rao use to evaluate the performance of her students?
opts: Percentage score
Ratios score
Decimal score
Fraction score
questions.explanation: A percentage score is used to evaluate performance in terms of a proportion of a total number of attempts.

questions.q: Why did Mrs. Rao decide to use a percentage score to evaluate the performance of her students?
opts: To make the assessment more time-consuming
To compare the performance of her students with national standards
To make the assessment more accurate and reliable
To make the assessment more subjective
questions.explanation: Using a percentage score makes the assessment more accurate and reliable as it provides a clear and consistent measure of performance.

questions.q: What was the range of scores recorded by Mrs. Rao for the 20 students?
questions.explanation: The scores ranged from 60% to 90%, indicating a moderate level of accuracy in free throws.

questions.q: What was Mrs. Rao's goal in conducting the basketball skills assessment?
opts: To compare the performance of her students with national standards
To improve her assessment methods to make it more accurate and reliable
To conduct a fun and engaging activity with her students
To measure the physical fitness of her students
questions.explanation: Mrs. Rao wanted to improve her assessment methods to make it more accurate and reliable, and also to compare the performance of her students with national standards.

questions.q: Why is it necessary to use a consistent and clear measure of performance in an assessment?
opts: To make the assessment more subjective
To make the assessment more time-consuming
To make the assessment more accurate and reliable
To compare the performance of students with national standards
questions.explanation: Using a consistent and clear measure of performance ensures that the assessment is accurate and reliable, and allows for meaningful comparisons to be made.

cases.passage: The Physical Education teacher, Mr. Kumar, was conducting a 400-meter dash event for his students. He had to measure the time taken by each student to complete the event. To calculate the average speed of the students, he recorded the total distance covered and the time taken by each student. The average speed of the students ranged from 8 km/h to 12 km/h. Mr. Kumar wanted to know how he could use the data to improve the performance of his students.

questions.q: What type of data did Mr. Kumar record to calculate the average speed of the students?
opts: Distance covered and time taken
Speed and distance covered
Time taken and average speed
Distance covered and average speed
questions.explanation: To calculate the average speed, Mr. Kumar recorded the total distance covered and the time taken by each student.

questions.q: What was the range of average speeds recorded by Mr. Kumar for the students?
opts: 6 km/h to 10 km/h
8 km/h to 12 km/h
10 km/h to 14 km/h
12 km/h to 16 km/h
questions.explanation: The average speeds ranged from 8 km/h to 12 km/h, indicating a moderate level of performance in the 400-meter dash event.

questions.q: Why is it necessary to use a consistent unit of measurement when recording data?
opts: To make the data more subjective
To make the data more time-consuming to record
To make the data more accurate and reliable
To make the data more difficult to analyze
questions.explanation: Using a consistent unit of measurement ensures that the data is accurate and reliable, and allows for meaningful comparisons to be made.

questions.q: What was Mr. Kumar's goal in conducting the 400-meter dash event?
opts: To improve the performance of his students
To conduct a fun and engaging activity with his students
To measure the physical fitness of his students
To compare the performance of his students with national standards
questions.explanation: Mr. Kumar wanted to improve the performance of his students by using the data to identify areas for improvement.

questions.q: Why is it necessary to analyze data in order to improve performance?
opts: To make the data more subjective
To make the data more time-consuming to record
To make the data more accurate and reliable
To identify areas for improvement
questions.explanation: Analyzing data allows for the identification of areas for improvement, which can be used to develop strategies for improvement.
